texto: The dream of true economic, gender and racial equality in a free society, which was cherished (if not achieved) by Leftists of the post-war generation, died under New Labour; but the egalitarianism at its heart was resurrected by a merciless minority as the brain-sucking zombie of Political Correctness.
explicacao: Citação de Mark Crutchfield sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “The dream of true economic, gender and racial equality in a free society, which was cherished (if…” articula motivacao, egalitarianism, equality em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e termino.

texto: Consciousness returns to its own dark thoughts and bad memories as reliably as kids to their own scabs, and maybe it's not so difficult to understand why. The mind doesn't like unresolved issues. Except that moods don't get resolved, they get forgotten – but just try forgetting the free-fall through depression's vacuum in a hurry. Worse than that, depression isn't just a memory, it's a state of mind. If you remember it, you're in it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mark Crutchfield: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Consciousness returns to its own dark thoughts and bad memories as reliably as kids to their own …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, depression, low-self-esteem pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e low-self-esteem.

texto: For the New Age community, 'ancient' knowledge is always considered unimpeachable and unimprovable, just as a diverse range of beliefs from Eastern mysticism to UFOs, energy dowsing to cryptozoology, are - though mutually contradictory - unquestionably accepted in the name of open-mindedness.
